288b819c2ca955a849a5947270c63254e58553be.summary: Keeping the top N lights per mesh in the culling phase.
288b819c2ca955a849a5947270c63254e58553be.body:
Previously, each mesh was paired up with the first N of each Omni- and Spot-lights.
Now each light is scored based on its distance to the mesh, energy, and range.
The new code uses a heap to track the best N lights (of each type) for predictability.

(cherry picked from commit 979d3e58d08d60261a90505787c1b22b87d01a01)

8c14a20871244a534b8d364f0c92304b87a9375a.summary: Fix game speed UI not resetting when game is restarted
8c14a20871244a534b8d364f0c92304b87a9375a.body:
_reset_time_scales() has a visibility guard that was originally needed
to prevent resets on session start. After 4e06e305bf scoped the call to
only run when all sessions end, the guard is unnecessary and prevents
the speed state from being properly reset.

Replace is_visible_in_tree() with an is_inside_tree() guard on the UI
update only, to avoid theme access warnings while still allowing the
state reset when the Game tab is not active.

Also includes a fix to the reset-to-1.0x button, which will now always be
disabled if the speed is already 1.0x.

Fixes #115400.

(cherry picked from commit d66c9cfebabbe9cf2aaad86b3664dcd6fe66e995)

d66c9cfebabbe9cf2aaad86b3664dcd6fe66e995.summary: Fix game speed UI not resetting when game is restarted

0f007ed768c0643a07425d35ace1f57b33d45863.body:
When two threads unreference() a RefCounted Object at the same time there's
a potential race.

Thread A sees rc_val == 1, enters the if() block.
Thread B sees rc_val == 0, enters the if() block.

Thread A gets scheduled out, or is simply scheduled on a slower core and
Thread B finishes, returning die = true. Thread B then frees the Object.

Some time during or after ~Object() Thread A wakes up and tries to call
methods on the Object, which is either destroyed or in the process of being
destroyed, leading to a use-after-free.

We fix the problem by counting how many threads are currently inside
the critical section, and blocking returning die = true as long as there are
still other threads potentially alive.

Tested by running with --test and by opening and playing various public and
private Godot projects.

This fixes #115173

(cherry picked from commit 6409ce6bb646e2c73362771b5e5e172de42a5a59)

6409ce6bb646e2c73362771b5e5e172de42a5a59.body:
When two threads unreference() a RefCounted Object at the same time there's
a potential race.

Thread A sees rc_val == 1, enters the if() block.
Thread B sees rc_val == 0, enters the if() block.

Thread A gets scheduled out, or is simply scheduled on a slower core and
Thread B finishes, returning die = true. Thread B then frees the Object.

Some time during or after ~Object() Thread A wakes up and tries to call
methods on the Object, which is either destroyed or in the process of being
destroyed, leading to a use-after-free.

We fix the problem by counting how many threads are currently inside
the critical section, and blocking returning die = true as long as there are
still other threads potentially alive.

Tested by running with --test and by opening and playing various public and
private Godot projects.

This fixes #115173
